tldr.text: Anthropic launched Claude Fable 5 and the restricted Claude Mythos 5, its most capable models ever, with Mythos reportedly breaching nearly all NSA classified systems in a red-team test.
Sarvam hit unicorn status with a $234M raise led by HCLTech, cementing India's push for sovereign AI infrastructure.

The Trump White House shelved a planned executive order on voluntary pre-release AI reviews after Musk and Zuckerberg objected, while quietly embedding commercial AI into classified military networks.

categories.name: Tools & Products
categories.blurb: The biggest model launches and developer tools hitting production this week.

stories.title: Anthropic's Fable 5 is out — and Mythos 5 reportedly broke the NSA
stories.summary: Anthropic launched Claude Fable 5, its most capable generally-available model yet, alongside the restricted Claude Mythos 5 for vetted cyberdefenders and government partners. Both are priced at $10 per million input tokens and $50 per million output tokens — less than half the cost of Mythos Preview. A separate report revealed that Mythos breached 'almost all' NSA classified systems within hours during a red-team evaluation, which preceded a Commerce Department directive limiting access.

stories.title: Google Gemma 4 12B: multimodal on your laptop, no encoders needed
stories.summary: Google released Gemma 4 12B, an open Apache 2.0 model that runs on 16GB of VRAM and accepts image, audio, and text inputs directly into its LLM backbone — no separate multimodal encoders required. It's positioned between the lightweight E4B and the larger 26B MoE model, with benchmark performance close to the 26B. Native audio input and Multi-Token Prediction drafters for lower latency make it the most capable mid-size open model Google has shipped.

stories.title: Google I/O recap: Gemini 3.5, Omni, Universal Cart, and the Googlebook
stories.summary: Google's May 2026 I/O blitz introduced Gemini 3.5 for complex agentic workflows and Gemini Omni for mixed-input video generation, alongside Android Halo for managing agents on-device. Hardware announcements included the Googlebook laptop built around Gemini Intelligence and the Fitbit Air health tracker. Universal Cart lets users add items across Search, YouTube, Gmail, and Gemini in one unified shopping flow.

stories.title: ChatGPT gets full-screen writing blocks and a clinician edition
stories.summary: OpenAI rolled out full-screen writing blocks in ChatGPT for long-form documents like essays, PRDs, and reports, with library saving, table of contents, and download support — available first to Plus and Pro users in the US. Separately, OpenAI launched ChatGPT for Clinicians, a free tier for verified US healthcare providers. GPT-5.5 Instant became the new default model for all ChatGPT users, replacing GPT-5.3 Instant.

stories.title: NVIDIA Alpamayo 2 Super scales AV reasoning to 32 billion parameters
stories.summary: NVIDIA expanded its open Alpamayo platform with Alpamayo 2 Super, a 32B-parameter vision-language-action model for autonomous vehicles with full 360-degree surround camera support and high-level meta-action outputs like yield and lane change. The release also includes AlpaGym, an open-source closed-loop reinforcement learning framework that scales from a single GPU to multi-node clusters. Alpamayo Recipes provides end-to-end workflow templates for fine-tuning, quantization, and deployment.

categories.name: Startups & Money
categories.blurb: This week's funding rounds, valuations, and the startups cashing in on the AI boom.

stories.title: Sarvam is India's newest AI unicorn at $1.5B
stories.summary: Bengaluru-based Sarvam raised $234M in a Series B led by HCLTech, which contributed $150M, with Bessemer, Khosla, and Peak XV also participating. The round values the company at $1.5 billion, making it India's newest AI unicorn as governments globally scramble for sovereign AI infrastructure. Sarvam recently released open-source models at 30B and 105B parameters and hopes to raise a total of $300M for the round.

stories.title: General Intuition in talks for $300M at $2B valuation
stories.summary: General Intuition, the New York startup building foundation models that teach AI agents spatial and temporal navigation through video game data, is in talks to raise roughly $300M, sources told TechCrunch. The raise would value the company at just over $2 billion, up from its $134M seed eight months ago. Jeff Bezos and Eric Schmidt have joined existing backers Khosla Ventures and General Catalyst in the new round.

stories.title: Swiss privacy AI startup Prem targets $500M valuation with $100M raise
stories.summary: Prem AI, based in Switzerland, is raising a $100M Series A targeting a valuation of at least $500 million. The company helps hedge funds and law firms run AI models entirely on private infrastructure, making it a bet on enterprise demand for sovereign, on-premise AI. The round is expected to close in Q3 2026.

stories.title: Pramaana Labs pulls $27M seed to bring formal verification to AI
stories.summary: Pramaana Labs announced a $27M seed round led by Khosla Ventures, with Accel, BoldCap, Nexus, Premji Invest, and Unbound also participating. The startup is applying formal verification methods — mathematical proofs of correctness — to AI systems, a niche that has gained urgency as agentic AI takes on higher-stakes tasks. The seed size signals serious institutional conviction in AI reliability tooling.

categories.name: The Bigger Picture
categories.blurb: The regulatory, legal, and geopolitical forces reshaping how AI gets built and governed.

stories.title: Trump's AI executive order shelved after Musk and Zuckerberg objected
stories.summary: The White House postponed signing an executive order that would have formalized voluntary pre-release government reviews of frontier AI models, after President Trump expressed concern it would block AI development — reportedly following lobbying from Elon Musk and Mark Zuckerberg. The administration simultaneously moved to embed commercial AI into classified military networks via deals with Google DeepMind, Microsoft, and xAI. The episode illustrates the growing tension between national security hawks pushing for AI oversight and industry figures resisting any friction on deployment.

stories.title: FDA-for-AI proposal dead; state patchwork accelerates
stories.summary: The Trump administration formally abandoned its 'FDA for AI' proposal on May 22, 2026, eliminating the prospect of a unified federal AI oversight framework. Connecticut enacted a comprehensive state AI law covering companion chatbots, frontier model governance, and employment AI, while Colorado repealed its landmark AI Act and replaced it with a lighter notice-and-disclosure regime. Louisiana became the 22nd US state to pass a consumer data privacy law, further expanding the compliance surface area for any company deploying AI.

stories.title: DOGE's ChatGPT-assisted grant cuts ruled unconstitutional
stories.summary: A federal court ruled that DOGE personnel violated constitutional equal protection and free speech rights when they used ChatGPT to terminate more than $100 million in National Endowment for the Humanities grants, finding that protected characteristics like race and gender were used as targeting criteria. The ruling is among the first to directly implicate AI tool use in a constitutional violation by a government actor. It sets a significant precedent for how AI-assisted government decision-making will be scrutinized by courts.

stories.title: AI prompts are now discoverable in federal litigation
stories.summary: In Conservation Law Foundation v. Shell Oil Company, a federal magistrate judge ordered plaintiffs to produce the AI prompts their expert used to prepare her report, ruling that prompts are part of an expert's methodology rather than protected drafting material. The decision marks one of the clearest signals yet that AI-assisted expert work will face full discovery scrutiny. Legal teams relying on AI for expert analysis now face a new and concrete evidentiary risk.
